title: Rule-Edit-Validate
description: JSON schema for creating or updating a Pega edit validate rule (Rule-Edit-Validate). Edit validate rules contain Java code that validates a single property value, returning true if valid or false (with an error message) if invalid.

pyRuleName.description: Internal rule key name, auto-derived from pyValidateName. PascalCase or camelCase, no spaces.

pyLabel.description: Human-readable display label for the rule.

pyValidateName.description: The validate name used to reference this rule from property definitions. Set manually; PascalCase or camelCase, no spaces.

pyClassName.description: The applies-to class for this rule. Often empty for global edit validates; set to a specific class to scope the validation.

pyDescription.description: Developer-facing description of the validation's purpose.

pyJavaCode.description: Java source code implementing the validation logic. Has access to 'theValue' (String), 'theProperty' (ClipboardProperty), 'tools' (PublicAPI), and 'pega' (PegaAPI). Must return boolean -- true if valid, false if invalid. Call theProperty.addMessage() to set error messages before returning false.

pyJavaStream.description: Alternative Java stream content. Typically empty -- use pyJavaCode instead.

pyMethodStatus.description: Method status controlling visibility and API classification.

pyCircumstanceDateProp.description: Property reference for date-based circumstance qualification.

pyCircumstanceTemplate.description: Template name for template-based circumstance qualification.

pyCircumstanceType.description: Type of circumstancing applied to this rule.
